titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 28

div.p: To all who shall view or hear this deed, Alexander of Stirling wishes eternal salvation in the Lord: May you all know that since the venerable father in Christ, David by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews, by the consent of the prior and religious community of the same place and for the augmentation of divine worship, and at my urging, granted to my chapel at Lauriston a chantry: lest the mother church of Egglisgrig should suffer any loss from such a grant, I promised faithfully that I and my heirs would cause its own expenses properly to be paid to the said chapel, and we would cause all obventions or offerings coming in to the said chapel to be transferred entirely to the said mother church of Egglisgrig, keeping it without injury or loss in all things, the chaplain also, whoever it be, that shall minister at the said chapel, before he celebrate the sacrament there shall take an oath of fealty to the said mother church, and as acknowledgement of submission, I and my heirs shall pay out one pound of wax each year on the Fair of Montrose in perpetuity. But if I or my heirs, or the chaplain who ministers in the said chapel, should act against any of the aforesaid things, I firmly promise for myself and my heirs that until appropriate satisfaction [has been made] there shall be no celebration of divine service in the aforesaid chapel. In testimony whereof together with the seal of the Archdeacon of St Andrews I have applied my seal to the present deed. Given at Ormiston on the Nones of December [5 December] in the year of grace 1243.
